title: triglochin huegelii
intro: Triglochin huegelii, also known as the sago lily or New Zealand sago, is a flowering plant species in the family Juncaginaceae. Native to Australia and New Zealand, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Triglochin huegelii include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant can be dried and used as a powder or tea, or made into a tincture, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
